conformance.default_intelligence_auth_path: codex-subscription through local Codex MCP server unless another admitted provider is selected
conformance.native_shell_posture: not exposed as native carrier shell in Agent Runtime Server mode; shell-like effects must be MCP tools and then admitted
conformance.mutating_call_handling: code-mediated Carrier Action Admission; read-only executes, mutating/unknown/credential-bearing routes or refuses
conformance.startup_sequence_availability: MCP tool if present in site fabric
known_gaps: Full owner-side canonical materialization remains outside this carrier boundary slice.

conformance.default_intelligence_auth_path: codex-subscription through local Codex MCP server unless another admitted provider is selected
conformance.native_shell_posture: not exposed as native carrier shell in Agent Runtime Server mode; shell-like effects must be MCP tools and then admitted
conformance.mutating_call_handling: code-mediated Carrier Action Admission; read-only executes, mutating/unknown/credential-bearing routes or refuses
conformance.startup_sequence_availability: MCP tool if present in site fabric; the Web UI projection is covered by separate live acceptance
known_gaps: Full owner-side canonical materialization remains outside this carrier boundary slice.

conformance.default_intelligence_auth_path: Provider selected by the agent-tui runtime configuration
conformance.mcp_fabric_source: Generated session MCP config consumed by agent-tui
conformance.native_shell_posture: The terminal runtime owns the interactive loop; Narada does not claim native shell mediation
conformance.mutating_call_handling: MCP surface policy applies to projected MCP calls; carrier-native terminal actions remain outside this boundary
conformance.startup_sequence_availability: MCP tool if present in the generated session MCP config

conformance.default_intelligence_auth_path: Codex subscription/auth handled by Codex CLI
conformance.mcp_fabric_source: Narada launcher projects Site MCP fabric into Codex mcp_servers arguments
conformance.native_shell_posture: Launcher passes --disable shell_tool unless break-glass EnableNativeShell is set
conformance.mutating_call_handling: Configuration limits available Narada MCP surfaces; Codex itself is not a Narada code-mediated admission loop
conformance.startup_sequence_availability: MCP tool if present in the projected fabric
known_gaps: Tool execution provenance is carrier-native unless routed through Narada-owned MCP/admission surfaces.
Codex approval settings are launcher configuration, not Agent Runtime Server code mediation.

conformance.mcp_fabric_source: No Narada MCP adapter is claimed; native carrier tools remain carrier-owned
conformance.native_shell_posture: Not enforced by Narada; Kimi manages its own tool execution
conformance.mutating_call_handling: No Narada code-mediated admission loop is claimed
conformance.startup_sequence_availability: No Narada startup MCP tool is claimed
known_gaps: Kimi native carrier integration has not been verified through a Narada adapter.

conformance.default_intelligence_auth_path: openai-codex provider/model via Pi CLI by launcher default
conformance.mcp_fabric_source: Narada-owned Pi extension loads Site MCP tools declared in .ai/mcp
conformance.native_shell_posture: Depends on Pi runtime; Narada launch attaches only the Site-local MCP bridge and governed prompt posture
conformance.mutating_call_handling: Configuration/adapter-mediated through Site MCP surfaces; not Agent Runtime Server code-mediated
conformance.startup_sequence_availability: MCP tool through the Narada Pi extension when the bridge loads correctly
known_gaps: Pi native carrier behavior is not universally intercepted by Agent Runtime Server; only Narada MCP bridge calls are governed by the projected Site surfaces.
Bridge startup should be smoke-tested per installed Pi version.

rows.admission_basis: Narada-owned Pi extension bridges Site-local .ai/mcp tools into Pi; MCP servers remain Site-local authority surfaces.

conformance.mcp_fabric_source: Narada launcher passes strict MCP config generated from Site fabric
conformance.mutating_call_handling: Claude Code effect mediation exists for explicit effect requests; native MCP calls are config-mediated unless adapter-mediated
conformance.startup_sequence_availability: MCP tool if present in generated strict MCP config
known_gaps: Not all Claude Code native tool behavior is Narada code-mediated.
Effect mediation is explicit artifact flow, not universal interception of all carrier actions.

conformance.default_intelligence_auth_path: No Narada intelligence provider; OpenCode manages its own provider/config
conformance.mcp_fabric_source: None; excluded from MCP fabric loading and uses ambient carrier tools
conformance.native_shell_posture: Not enforced by Narada; OpenCode manages its own tool execution
conformance.mutating_call_handling: No Narada code-mediated admission loop is claimed
conformance.startup_sequence_availability: Not available as a Narada MCP tool; the launcher injects a startup prompt
known_gaps: OpenCode is a pass-through carrier; Narada does not inject MCP tools.
Shell execution provenance is carrier-native, not Narada code-mediated.

rows.admission_basis: The current OpenCode launcher injects a startup prompt but does not inject or verify a Narada MCP configuration. Native MCP attachment remains a separate integration milestone.
